data.title: RED: Write failing tests for EAGAIN retry in suite-engine.sh
data.description:
## Description

Create tests/hooks/test-suite-engine-eagain.sh with behavioral tests for EAGAIN detection and retry in suite-engine.sh. Tests use a mock test script that simulates EAGAIN failures. All tests MUST fail (RED) before implementation.

## Test Approach

Create mock test scripts in temp dirs. Each mock controls its exit code and stderr output. Use run_test_suite (from suite-engine.sh) to execute the mock test. Verify retry behavior by checking output, exit codes, and MAX_PARALLEL value during retry.

The mock runner for retry tests should use a state file: exit 254 + EAGAIN on first call, exit 0 on second call. This proves retry happened and succeeded.

## Test Functions

1. test_eagain_retry_on_exit254_with_pattern: Mock test exits 254, outputs "fork: Resource temporarily unavailable". Verify suite-engine retries (mock passes on second call via state file). Verify MAX_PARALLEL=1 was exported during retry.

2. test_no_retry_exit254_without_pattern: Mock exits 254, no EAGAIN in output. Verify no retry occurs (mock only called once).

3. test_no_retry_with_pattern_wrong_exit: Mock exits 1, outputs EAGAIN pattern. Verify no retry (exit code must be 254).

4. test_retry_result_is_authoritative: Mock exits 254 + EAGAIN, retry passes. Verify final test result is PASS (not FAIL or TOLERATED).

5. test_retry_failure_falls_through: Mock exits 254 + EAGAIN on every call. Verify final result is FAIL.

6. test_eagain_blocking_io_pattern: Mock exits 254, outputs "BlockingIOError: [Errno 35] Resource temporarily unavailable". Verify retry triggers (alternate pattern).

## Files to Modify
- tests/hooks/test-suite-engine-eagain.sh (CREATE)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file exists
  Verify: test -f tests/hooks/test-suite-engine-eagain.sh
- [ ] Contains at least 6 test functions
  Verify: grep -c "^test_" tests/hooks/test-suite-engine-eagain.sh | awk '{exit ($1 < 6)}'
- [ ] All tests FAIL against current code (RED)
  Verify: bash tests/hooks/test-suite-engine-eagain.sh 2>&1; test $? -ne 0
- [ ] No syntax errors
  Verify: bash -n tests/hooks/test-suite-engine-eagain.sh
